mnemonics.title: Agile Tool Categories
mnemonics.content: JTV CTC - Jira, Trello, Version control, CI/CD, Testing, Collaboration

mnemonics.title: CI/CD Pipeline Steps
mnemonics.content: Build Test Deploy - B-T-D (Better Test Deploy)

acronyms.usage: Automates software delivery pipeline from code commit to production deployment
The amount of work currently being handled, limited in Kanban to improve focus
Testing approach using natural language specifications (Gherkin) with tools like Cucumber
Shared checklist that determines when a task is considered complete
Used for desktop virtualization in enterprise Agile environments

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a factory assembly line with stages: Raw Materials (To Do) → Manufacturing (In Progress) → Quality Check (Review) → Finished Products (Done)
Think of a water pipeline where code flows through filters (tests) and emerges clean at the end (production deployment)
Visualize a staircase descending from top-left to bottom-right, where each step represents completed work approaching the goal line
Imagine a pyramid with most important items at the top (urgent) and least important at the bottom

analogies.analogy: Like a time machine for code - you can go back to any point and see exactly what changed, who changed it, and why
Like an automated factory assembly line that inspects, assembles, and packages products without manual intervention
Like a restaurant's order tracking system showing orders being prepared, cooking, and served
Like a shopping list for a specific week's groceries (sprint) chosen from the master grocery list (product backlog)
Like shipping containers - standardized boxes that can be moved anywhere and guarantee contents arrive exactly as packed
